definition: Gold's post-2022 rally represents a structural regime break driven by central bank reserve diversification following the Russian reserve freeze. The historical real-rate model (R² ~0.7) broke down as real yields rose from -1% to +2% while gold rallied from $1,800 to $2,400+. Central bank purchases of ~1,000t/year create a demand floor of ~20-25% of annual mine supply.
evidence: Real-rate model (gold inversely correlated with TIPS, R² ~0.7 historically) broke down in 2022-2023
Real yields rose from -1% to +2% while gold rallied from $1,800 to $2,400+
Central bank gold purchases: 1,037t in 2023, ~1,050t in 2024, ~900-1,000t annualized 2025-2026
Demand floor of ~20-25% of annual mine supply from official sector
Sanctions → diversification → gold causal chain well-supported
Non-G3 buyers (PBoC, NBP, RBI, MAS) driving the structural shift
open_questions: What is the price elasticity of official-sector gold demand at $3,000+?
Is the regime break permanent or will real-rate correlations reassert at some threshold?
Which non-G3 central banks will sustain purchasing programs long-term?
Does gold function as a pressure release valve for permanent balance sheet expansion?
